articles.title: Gold, silver prices today: Comex gold and silver extend rally as weak US jobs data cools Fed rate hike fears
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices rose on 3 July as easing rate hike fears followed a weak US jobs report. Comex gold futures climbed to $4,208, while silver futures reached $63.50. Employment data indicated a slowdown, lowering rate-hike expectations and negatively impacting the dollar.
articles.content:
Gold and silver prices extended their recovery rally on Friday, 3 July, as fears of an imminent interest rate hike by the US Federal Reserve eased following a softer-than-expected US jobs report. The weaker data also tempered the rally in the dollar, making dollar-denominated commodities more attractive to holders of other currencies.

Comex gold futures climbed $83 per troy ounce to an intraday high of $4,208 and were on track for a second consecutive weekly gain. Silver futures advanced $2.35 per troy ounce to $63.50.

Data released on Thursday showed that US hiring slowed sharply in June, suggesting the labour market continues to face challenges despite signs of resilience in recent months.

The US economy added just 57,000 jobs in June, the fewest in four months and well below forecasts of 110,000, while the unemployment rate stood at 4.2%. The report followed weaker-than-expected private-sector employment data released on Wednesday.

The softer labour market numbers are expected to reduce pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise rates at its July meeting. Expectations of higher borrowing costs have been a key headwind for non-yielding bullion.

Earlier this week, Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation expectations and inflation risks had moderated in recent weeks, while reaffirming the central bank’s commitment to bringing inflation back to its 2% target.

As traders scaled back expectations of a near-term Fed rate hike, demand for the US dollar also weakened. The dollar index slipped below 101 in Friday’s trade and was headed for its biggest weekly decline since April.

Oil prices, a major driver of inflation in recent months, have also retreated towards pre-war levels as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has increased. Crude prices have edged lower this week following positive discussions in Qatar aimed at converting the US-Iran interim 60-day truce into a lasting peace agreement.

Meanwhile, gold ended the second quarter of 2026 with a 13.5% decline, marking its worst quarterly performance in 13 years. The metal also snapped a six-quarter winning streak, during which it had rallied 76%, after touching a record high of $5,626 in January.

Tracking gains in the international market, the near-month gold futures contract on the MCX surged ₹2,311 per 10 grams to an intraday high of ₹1,48,069, extending its winning streak to a fourth consecutive session. The rebound came after the metal ended June with an 8% decline.

“Market participants will now closely watch next week’s Federal Reserve meeting minutes for further guidance on the US interest-rate outlook and the dollar’s direction. Technically, gold is expected to trade in the ₹1,45,000–1,49,000 range, with global cues continuing to drive sentiment,” said Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst – Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities.

Meanwhile, the near-month silver futures contract on the MCX climbed ₹5,572 per kilogram to touch the day’s high of ₹2,38,876.

Disclaimer: The views and recommendations made above are those of individual analysts or broking companies, and not of Mint. We advise investors to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.

Ksheera Sagar has been working as a Market Research Analyst at LiveMint for the past four years, covering stocks, commodities, and broader financial markets. In this role, he closely tracks daily market movements, corporate earnings, sector trends, and macroeconomic developments. <br><br> He has over a decade of experience in the financial services industry and has previously worked with multiple organisations, including global investment bank J.P. Morgan, bringing strong research experience into the newsroom. <br><br> During his career, he has gained extensive exposure to equity research, market analysis, and financial data interpretation, strengthening his expertise across asset classes and market cycles. <br><br> He is known for his data-driven analysis and crisp, listicle-style market stories that break down complex financial developments across key markets for a wide audience. His strong research skills enable him to write detailed and insightful stories on stocks and sectors, focusing on the underlying factors driving market movements. <br><br> His work combines quantitative insights with clear storytelling, presenting financial developments in a clear and structured manner. Moreover, he enjoys writing multibagger and listicle-style copies. Outside of work, Ksheera enjoys playing the piano and exploring new places. He has a keen interest in travel, music, and continuously learning about global markets and economic trends.

Catch all the Business News , Market News , Breaking News Events and Latest News Updates on Live Mint. Download The Mint News App to get Daily Market Updates.

articles.title: Govt directs Apple, Google to remove 7 apps allegedly misused to disable e-rickshaw batteries, says report—Latest update
articles.summary: MeitY has directed Google and Apple to remove seven apps linked to battery shutdowns in e-rickshaws following complaints of operational disruptions faced by drivers. Here are the latest developments…
articles.content:
The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has today directed Google and Apple to remove seven apps from the Android Play Store and iOS App Store respectively for their alleged misuse in shutting down batteries in e-rickshaws and other vehicles, ANI reported citing sources.

According to sources, the action pertains to applications that were allegedly being misused to disable batteries installed in e-rickshaws and vehicles.

Quick answers to key questions

The Indian government has directed the removal of seven apps, including BAT-BMS, SMART BMS, and LOSSIGY, from app stores for allegedly being misused to disable e-rickshaw batteries.

The action was prompted by complaints from e-rickshaw drivers about sudden stoppages and operational disruptions caused by the apps, raising safety concerns for both drivers and passengers.

These apps exploit security gaps in unsecured lithium-ion battery packs that connect via Bluetooth, allowing any nearby smartphone to turn off the battery without password protection.

Creating and using these apps to disable e-rickshaws is considered a criminal offense under section 66 and section 43 of the Information Technology Act 2000, carrying penalties of up to 3 years imprisonment and fines of ₹5 lakh.

The government is pushing app stores to exercise greater diligence in reviewing apps to ensure they do not pose risks to public safety or facilitate unlawful activities.

The action comes after reports over the past few days of sudden stoppages and operational disruptions faced by e-rickshaw drivers. These applications were allegedly being used to interfere with the functioning of certain e-rickshaws, leading to unexpected interruptions in their operations and raising safety concerns for both drivers and passengers.

The Centre took serious note of the issue after complaints emerged from e-rickshaw operators regarding unexplained disruptions affecting their vehicles. The alleged misuse of the applications prompted authorities to examine the matter and take corrective action.

Explaining the issue, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), S Krishnan also said on Friday, “There are a couple of apps which came up to our notice yesterday. Both of them have been taken down from the app stores”.

He further stated that app stores have a responsibility to ensure that applications made available to users do not pose risks to public safety or facilitate unlawful activities.

“The idea is that this is due care that the app stores have to exercise and we will take it up with the app stores to see that possibly damaging apps do not come up,” he said.

India has witnessed rapid growth in the use of electric rickshaws as an affordable and environmentally friendly mode of transport, particularly for last-mile connectivity in urban and semi-urban areas.

The expansion of digital services and smartphone-based applications has also increased the need for stronger safeguards against malicious software that could compromise vehicles or enable fraud.

The government has not officially disclosed further technical details about the functioning of the applications.

Notably, the government action follows reports doing the rounds on social media where viral videos showed pranksters switching off the vehicles mid-journey using mobile applications.

In one such video, a distressed e-rickshaw driver who complained about hours of forced downtime deadlock due to the misuse of such apps. He said the rented vehicle was his only source of income.

The widespread use of these apps for views on Instagram, YouTube, Reddit and X has brought in disastrous consequences for e-rickshaw drivers and disrupted traffic. According to HT report, the disabled e-rickshaw cannot be activated again unless the action is reversed through the app rather than the vehicle’s own key.

Chairman of the International Commission on Cyber Security Law, Pawan Duggal in an interview with ANI warned that legal action would be taken against perpetrators. “I am very clear this is not a game this is an offense under section 66 read with section 43 of the Information Technology Act 2000 because this is an activity that's done dishonestly or fraudulently where people enter into the computer system of its e-rickshaw without the consent or the knowledge of the owner and that is punishable with 3 years imprisonment and a fine worth ₹5 lakh,” Duggal said.

(With inputs from ANI)

articles.title: Pulse of the Street: Realty and healthcare lifts markets, but weak rupee clouds rally
articles.summary: Markets rose nearly 1% this week as softer crude oil prices, easing volatility and buying in real estate, healthcare and consumption stocks lifted sentiment. However, weakness in capital goods and power, along with continued pressure on the rupee, showed that the recovery rema...
articles.content:
Indian markets rose nearly 1% this week as softer crude oil prices, easing volatility and buying in real estate, healthcare and consumption stocks lifted sentiment. However, weakness in capital goods and power, along with continued pressure on the rupee, showed that the recovery remained selective, and would need support from June-quarter earnings to sustain.

On Friday, the Sensex rose 0.34% to close at 77,763.94, while the Nifty 50 advanced 0.39% to 24,270.85. Volatility eased during the week, with the fear gauge index India VIX falling to 11.79 from 13.05. Besides, crude oil prices continued their slide to $72.70 a barrel from $75.07, offering some relief to an economy that depends heavily on energy imports, while the rupee remained under pressure, weakening 0.92% to 95.223 against the dollar.

Realty lead

Realty stocks led the market rally, with the BSE Realty index surging 7.8% during the week. Healthcare gained 3.1%, while consumer durables and consumer discretionary stocks advanced about 2% each.

However, the gains were not broad-based. Capital goods stocks declined 2.7%, while the power index fell 2.6%. Sectors such as oil and gas and telecommunications slipped 0.4% and 0.2%, respectively.

Said Tanvi Kanchan, associate director at Anand Rathi Share and Stock Brokers, “Gains in realty, healthcare and consumer stocks reflected easing geopolitical concerns, cooling crude prices and improving domestic demand expectations. Weakness in capital goods and power, appeared to be profit-booking rather than a structural downturn.”

“Cyclicals could participate in the next phase if June-quarter earnings confirm resilient demand and healthy order books,” Kanchan added.

Rajesh Singla, founder and chief executive of Alpha AMC, called it sector rotation rather than a broad uptrend. “Lower crude prices and dovish Fed commentary have supported realty and consumption, while capital goods and power are seeing profit-booking. Earnings will determine whether the rally can sustain,” he said.

Singla expects IT, realty and domestic financials to remain in focus, with US jobs data, FPI flows and earnings commentary guiding the market next week.

Relative resilience

Worldwide, Indian equities performed better than several Asian and emerging-market peers during the week. India outperformed Indonesia, China, Brazil and South Korea, where benchmark indices declined 0.3-4%. However, it lagged Taiwan, Germany, Hong Kong and the S&P 500, which gained between 2% and 5%.

Kanchan said Taiwan’s outperformance was largely driven by artificial intelligence and semiconductor stocks, sectors that have limited weight in Indian benchmark indices. The Nasdaq’s gains also reflected a rebound in technology stocks after weeks of selling.

“Monsoon progress, crude oil prices and June-quarter earnings will be the key domestic triggers,” she added. “Softer crude would remain supportive for India’s inflation, currency and fiscal outlook.”

Rupee under pressure

Meanwhile, the rupee emerged as the second-worst-performing currency among the global peers, weakening 0.92% to 95.223 against the green back from 94.40 a week earlier. Only the Russian ruble fared worse, depreciating 2.65%. Brazilian real and Indonesian rupiah were the other two weak performing currencies depreciating 0.46% and 0.19%, respectively during the week. The ones performing well included the Malaysian ringgit which appreciated 1.2%, followed by the Mexican peso and Japanese yen.

Kunal Sodhani, head of treasury at Shinhan Bank India highlighted that the rupee’s sharp underperformance was driven not only by broad dollar strength, but also by the RBI’s large short forward dollar position, which has kept the spot market relatively under-hedged and encouraged speculative pressure. “As these forward positions are gradually unwound, demand for dollars in the spot market could keep the rupee under pressure.”

“Prolonged depreciation may also weigh on FPI sentiment and domestic equities if investors perceive higher currency risk and imported inflation,” he added.

articles.title: Western Digital Corp outlines its storage strategy as investors watch the US technology sector
articles.summary: Western Digital Corp is refining its data storage strategy, focusing on long-term demand for cloud and device storage solutions amidst investor interest in the US technology sector. The company specializes in hard disk drives and solid-state drives for both consumer and enterp...
articles.content:
Western Digital Corp (ISIN US95040Q1040) remains one of the established names in global data storage, building on decades of experience in hard disk drives, solid-state drives and related solutions for consumer devices and enterprise infrastructure.

Storage specialist with global reach

The company develops and sells storage products used in personal computers, game consoles, mobile devices and external storage solutions, while also supplying components for servers and data centers operated by large technology platforms.

Western Digital Corp's business model combines manufacturing capabilities with design and firmware expertise, enabling it to offer both branded products and components supplied to other manufacturers and system integrators.

Enterprise and cloud demand

In the enterprise segment, Western Digital Corp focuses on high-capacity drives and flash-based solutions that support cloud service providers and corporate IT infrastructure, reflecting the continuing growth of data creation and storage requirements around the world.

Analysts often highlight how demand for data storage in cloud computing, artificial intelligence workloads and streaming services can underpin long-term orders for enterprise-grade hard drives and solid-state drives.

Consumer product portfolio

Beyond large-scale infrastructure, Western Digital Corp offers a broad range of consumer-oriented devices under several established storage brands, including internal drives for desktop and notebook PCs, external portable drives and flash-based storage accessories designed for everyday use.

Western Digital Corp stock context

Western Digital Corp is listed in the United States and its shares are commonly included in discussions of the wider US technology and semiconductor-related hardware sector, giving investors exposure to trends in data growth, device shipments and enterprise infrastructure spending.

articles.title: Quest Diagnostics highlights its role in US healthcare. The stock reflects a stable diagnostics busi
articles.summary: The article highlights Quest Diagnostics' significant role in the US healthcare system as a leading provider of diagnostic information services. It details the company's business model, which relies on high-volume clinical laboratory testing, strong payer relationships, and ad...
articles.content:
Quest Diagnostics is a major provider of diagnostic information services in the United States, delivering laboratory testing for physicians, hospitals, employers and patients across a broad range of medical conditions. The company (ISIN US74834L1008) plays an important role in supporting clinical decisions, public health initiatives and preventive care through its nationwide network of laboratories and patient service centers.

Large-scale lab testing operations

The core of Quest Diagnostics' business is high-volume clinical laboratory testing, where samples from physician offices, clinics and hospitals are processed in regional and national laboratories. Its menu spans routine tests such as blood chemistry panels and complete blood counts, as well as more specialized diagnostics covering oncology, infectious disease, cardiometabolic disorders and genetic screening. These services help clinicians confirm diagnoses, monitor treatment effectiveness and detect disease risk early.

To support this model, Quest Diagnostics operates centralized laboratories combined with a logistics network that moves millions of specimens each year from collection sites to testing facilities. The company relies on standardized processes, automation and quality-control systems to maintain accuracy and turnaround times. This scale allows it to spread fixed costs over large testing volumes, which can support margin stability when volumes are healthy.

Payer relationships and revenue mix

Quest Diagnostics generates much of its revenue through contracts with health insurers and government programs, supplemented by direct arrangements with hospitals, health systems and employers. Reimbursement terms influence profitability, so maintaining and renewing payer agreements is a central strategic task. Over time, the company has expanded its presence in managed care networks, which can help secure patient access and support steady volumes.

The company also benefits from relationships with large health systems that outsource parts of their laboratory operations. These arrangements typically involve multi-year contracts, under which Quest Diagnostics provides testing services, manages laboratories or supports outreach programs. Such partnerships can add predictable revenue streams and deepen integration into local care ecosystems.

Digital ordering and data services

Digital infrastructure is another important pillar of Quest Diagnostics' strategy. Electronic ordering platforms enable physicians and clinics to submit test requests, track specimen status and retrieve results, often integrated directly into electronic health record systems. This connectivity reduces administrative friction, helps minimize errors related to manual data entry, and can speed clinical decision-making.

Beyond delivering test results, Quest Diagnostics aggregates diagnostic data across its network. De-identified information can support population health analyses, research collaborations and quality-improvement initiatives. Such data assets, handled under privacy and regulatory frameworks, contribute to the company's position as a key information provider in the US healthcare system.

Regulatory and quality frameworks

Laboratory operations are subject to extensive regulatory oversight, including certification requirements and quality standards. Quest Diagnostics laboratories operate under regulations that define how tests are validated, how equipment is maintained and how staff are trained. Internal quality programs, proficiency testing and audits help ensure that results are reliable across locations and over time.

Compliance with these frameworks is critical not only for patient safety but also for maintaining contracts with hospitals and payers. Any significant quality issue can have financial, reputational and legal consequences, which is why large diagnostic companies invest heavily in compliance systems, laboratory information management and staff education.

Growth drivers in diagnostics

Several structural trends support demand for diagnostic testing. An aging population typically requires more monitoring for chronic conditions, increasing orders for routine panels and specialized tests. Rising prevalence of cardiometabolic disorders, such as diabetes and hyperlipidemia, also leads to regular laboratory monitoring to guide therapy and assess risk.

Preventive medicine and wellness programs often include laboratory components, for example screening for lipid levels, thyroid function or certain cancers. Employers and health plans may sponsor wellness initiatives that rely on lab results to identify health risks and encourage lifestyle changes. As a major provider of these tests, Quest Diagnostics is positioned to participate in such programs.

Innovation and test menu expansion

Maintaining a competitive test menu requires continuous evaluation of new assays and methodologies. Diagnostic companies review emerging technologies, such as advanced molecular diagnostics, next-generation sequencing and novel biomarkers, to determine which offerings align with clinical demand and reimbursement viability. Adding new tests can open incremental revenue streams and differentiate services for specialists.

Quest Diagnostics evaluates clinical evidence, guideline updates and customer feedback when deciding to introduce or expand specific tests. For example, growing attention to hereditary cancer risk or pharmacogenomics has led many laboratories to offer relevant panels. The balance lies in expanding the menu without overextending into areas where demand or reimbursement are uncertain.

Laboratory reach and patient access

Physical access is a practical consideration in the diagnostics business. Quest Diagnostics operates patient service centers where individuals can have blood drawn or specimens collected, often located near physician offices or within retail settings. Convenient locations and extended operating hours can improve adherence when physicians order tests that require fasting or multiple appointments.

The company also offers home collection options in some circumstances, leveraging logistics partners and kit-based sampling. These models gained more attention as remote care and telehealth expanded, since they allow patients to complete necessary laboratory work without visiting traditional clinical settings. Combining in-person and remote options helps the company cover different patient preferences.

Role in infectious disease testing

Infectious disease diagnostics are a meaningful part of laboratory work, covering conditions ranging from common respiratory pathogens to sexually transmitted infections and hospital-acquired infections. Large laboratories provide both routine tests and specialized panels based on molecular methodologies, such as PCR and related techniques. This capacity supports clinicians in identifying pathogens, guiding antibiotic use and monitoring outbreaks.

Quest Diagnostics' network is capable of scaling infectious disease testing during periods of heightened demand, supporting public health responses and healthcare system needs. Laboratory capacity planning, inventory management and staffing flexibility are important for navigating fluctuations in test volumes driven by seasonal patterns or emerging health concerns.

Chronic disease management support

For chronic conditions like diabetes, chronic kidney disease and cardiovascular disease, laboratory results are integral to ongoing management. Physicians rely on metrics such as HbA1c, kidney function markers and lipid profiles to adjust medications and assess risk. By providing these tests at scale, Quest Diagnostics contributes to long-term care pathways that can reduce complications if managed effectively.

Diagnostic companies also work with health plans and care management programs to identify patients who may benefit from targeted interventions based on lab trends. Such collaborations can support value-based care initiatives that align financial incentives with improved outcomes, although specific arrangements vary by region and payer.

Competitive landscape in diagnostics

The US clinical laboratory market includes large national providers, regional laboratories and hospital-based labs. Quest Diagnostics competes with other national networks, as well as numerous local entities that sometimes specialize in particular disease areas. Competition can occur on pricing, service quality, turnaround times and access to advanced tests.

To differentiate, large providers emphasize comprehensive test menus, broad geographic reach, integrated digital platforms and specialized services for complex diagnostics. They may also pursue partnerships with health systems, academic centers and technology companies to expand capabilities. In this environment, scale can be an advantage, but local relationships and service responsiveness remain important.

Long-term strategic priorities

Over the long term, Quest Diagnostics focuses on maintaining and growing testing volumes, optimizing cost structures and investing in innovation. Operational efficiency initiatives can include automation, process redesign and consolidation of certain laboratory functions to reduce per-test costs. At the same time, customer-facing improvements, such as streamlined ordering workflows and clearer result reporting, aim to strengthen client loyalty.

Strategic priorities also encompass portfolio management, where the company may adjust its mix of routine and esoteric tests, pursue specialty offerings in areas with strong clinical evidence, and evaluate potential collaborations or acquisitions. Balancing routine high-volume work with specialized, higher-value tests can support both revenue stability and growth opportunities.

Representative diagnostics offering

One representative category in Quest Diagnostics' portfolio is comprehensive blood chemistry testing, which aggregates multiple analytes into panels that inform a physician about a patient's metabolic status, organ function and risk factors. These panels often include measures related to liver enzymes, kidney function, electrolytes, glucose and lipids. As a standard component of many medical evaluations, they illustrate how routine diagnostics underpin everyday clinical decisions.

Quest Diagnostics stock and listing

Quest Diagnostics is listed on a major US stock exchange and its shares trade in US dollars, reflecting investor interest in the diagnostics and laboratory services sector. The stock's performance over time tends to be influenced by testing volumes, reimbursement trends, operational efficiency and broader healthcare utilization patterns.

For investors, the company represents exposure to a business model tied to recurring healthcare needs and long-term demographic trends, rather than to short-lived product cycles. Market participants often compare its valuation and performance with other healthcare service providers and diagnostic firms when assessing relative opportunities and risks in the sector.

Quest Diagnostics Inc. is recognized as a leading provider of diagnostic information services in the US healthcare system, operating extensive laboratory networks that process millions of tests each year. Its business model combines high-volume routine testing with specialized diagnostics and data-driven services.

articles.title: H.C. Wainwright Remains a Buy on Biogen (BIIB)
articles.summary: H.C. Wainwright maintained a Buy rating on Biogen (BIIB) with a price target of $237.00. This rating was echoed by Needham's Ami Fadia, while Morgan Stanley holds a Neutral stance. Biogen reported increased quarterly revenue and net profit in its latest earnings release for Q1...
articles.content:
In a report released yesterday, Ananda Ghosh from H.C. Wainwright maintained a Buy rating on Biogen, with a price target of $237.00.

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

According to TipRanks, Ghosh is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 26.2% and a 60.44% success rate. Ghosh covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Maze Therapeutics, Inc., ProQR, and PepGen Inc..

In addition to H.C. Wainwright, Biogen also received a Buy from Needham’s Ami Fadia in a report issued on June 29. However, on June 18, Morgan Stanley maintained a Hold rating on Biogen (NASDAQ: BIIB).

Based on Biogen’s latest earnings release for the quarter ending March 31, the company reported a quarterly revenue of $2.48 billion and a net profit of $319.5 million. In comparison, last year the company earned a revenue of $2.43 billion and had a net profit of $240.5 million

articles.title: Bessent dismisses concerns over Trump's billions in crypto earnings: 'I don't think there's an appearance problem'
articles.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated that President Trump's recent crypto earnings of $1.4 billion do not present a conflict of interest, despite Democratic criticism. Details here.
articles.content:
US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said he does not believe the recent disclosure of President Donald Trump's billions in crypto earnings poses a problem for the leader, Bessent said in an interview with CBS News on Thursday.

"I don't think there's an appearance problem," he said referring to Trump's earnings from the digital asset.

The remarks come after a financial disclosure released earlier this week showed that Trump reported earning approximately $1.4 billion in 2025 from investments linked to cryptocurrencies and memecoins.

Congressional Democrats have criticised Trump's crypto windfall, arguing it presents a conflict of interest since his administration has sought to ease regulations on cryptocurrency, CBS News reported.

During his presidency, Trump has reportedly championed a pro-cryptocurrency agenda, saying the United States should become the “crypto capital of the world” by easing regulations, creating a strategic Bitcoin reserve and promoting the use of dollar-backed stablecoins.

"This is an innovation presidency," Bessent told the broadcaster. "So whether it's digital access, whether it's AI, whether it's everything that is going on in the tech ecosystem that, you know, all Americans are benefiting from that."

White House spokesperson Anna Kelly told CBS News on Tuesday that "there are no conflicts of interest" in the disclosure.

Trump's holdings include his "meme coin" $TRUMP and earnings from World Liberty Financial, a cryptocurrency company backed by the president and his family, according to his financial disclosure filings.

The president reported earning approximately $594 million from World Liberty Financial and about $636 million in income from his memecoin business. He also made nearly $197 million from an equity sale involving Stablecoin Holdco.

While some critics have raised concerns over a potential conflict of interest, Trump claimed on Thursday that he was not fully aware of the size of his family's cryptocurrency holdings. He also maintained that his association with the crypto ventures was entirely legal following disclosures of significant profits from family-linked digital asset businesses.

“I could know about it. I didn’t," Trump mentioned in an interview at the White House.

He also told CNBC: "I’ve always made money. I’m a business person. I’m a really good business person. I made money. I made a tremendous amount of money.”

When questioned about the billions of dollars he had earned from cryptocurrency ventures, Trump dismissed concerns over a potential conflict of interest, in line with what Bessent said too during an interview with CBS News.

“The way I view crypto is a little differently. We have to be at the top, otherwise China is going to take it over,” he said.

The US president also said he does not personally manage his crypto investments, stating that they are handled on his behalf. He added that he is not aware of the identities of the investors involved.

“I let people invest it. I don’t even speak to – I don’t even know who they are. My son Eric handles it. I don’t talk to him about things such as this," he further said.

articles.title: Dow Hits 21st All-Time Closing High in 2026: Buy These 3 Stocks
articles.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average has reached its 21st all-time closing high in 2026, driven by investors seeking defensive assets amidst a tech selloff. The index gained 8.9% in the first half of the year, its best performance since 2021, and is up 10.1% year-to-date. Goldman...
articles.content:
Dow Hits 21st All-Time Closing High in 2026: Buy These 3 Stocks

Wall Street has turned volatile over the past two weeks, led by a massive tech selloff. However, the Dow has had a solid run this year, with the blue-chip index hitting a new all-time closing high on Thursday.

After a stellar first half of 2026, the Dow started the second half on a high, thanks to investors who have lately been taking refuge in defensive assets amid an AI-related tech selloff.

Given the positive sentiment, investors should bet on these three fundamentally strong blue-chip stocks, namely The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.GS, Caterpillar Inc.CAT and The Coca-Cola CompanyKO. These companies are well-positioned to benefit from the market’s overall upward trend, offering potential for solid returns.

Dow on a Rally

The Dow gained 1.1%, or 594.83 points, to close at a record high of 52,900.07 on Thursday. The milestone was achieved on the second trading day of the second half of the year. This is also the 21st record closing high for the blue-chip index this year.

While the Nasdaq has been weighed down lately by a massive selloff in tech stocks, the Dow has gained as investors are rotating out of AI-related stocks and taking refuge in defensive assets. Concerns over the sustainability of AI-related stocks have been dampening investors’ confidence.

The blue-chip index suffered in the early months of the year after the U.S-Iran war broke out in late February. However, easing geopolitical tensions over the past month led to a dip in global oil prices, helping the index.

Also, robust corporate earnings by industry bellwethers in the first two quarters fueled the Dow rally in the first half of the year.

The Dow gained 8.9% in the first half of 2026, to record its best first half since 2021. The index has gained nearly 10.1% year to date after rising nearly 13% in 2025.

With the Iran war likely to come to an end soon, inflation is expected to ease. Till some time back, the Federal Reserve was contemplating a rate hike this year as inflation surged on higher oil prices over the past few months. However, rate hike worries eased earlier this week, with the Fed indicating that inflation may decline in the near term.

3 Dow Stocks With Upside

The Goldman Sachs Group

The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. is a leading global financial holding company providing IB, securities, investment management and consumer banking services to a diversified client base. GS is headquartered in New York, with offices in major financial centers globally.

The Goldman Sachs Group’s expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 17.3%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 1.9% over the past 60 days. Currently, GS has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.

Caterpillar

Caterpillar Inc. is the largest global construction and mining equipment manufacturer. Given that it serves a gamut of sectors — infrastructure, construction, mining, oil & gas and transportation, CAT is considered a bellwether of the global economy. Caterpillar has more than 4 million products with an extensive dealer network of 165 dealers spanning 191 countries.

Caterpillar’s expected earnings growth for the current year is 29.4%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 3.4% over the past 60 days. CAT currently carries a Zacks Rank #1.

The Coca-Cola Company

The Coca-Cola Company’s strong brand equity, marketing, research and innovation help it to garner a market share of more than 40% in the non-alcoholic beverage industry. KO is putting its best foot forward to evolve its business model to become a total beverage company with something for everyone to drink.

The Coca-Cola Company has an expected earnings growth rate of 8.7% for the current year. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 0.3% over the past 60 days. The Coca-Cola Company currently has a Zacks Rank #2.

Beyond Nvidia: AI's Second Wave Is Here

The AI revolution has already minted millionaires. But the stocks everyone knows about aren't likely to keep delivering the biggest profits. AI’s second wave is moving from infrastructure to implementation and these companies are at the forefront of this transition, positioned to become what Amazon and Google were to the internet era.

See Stocks Now >>

articles.title: Synchrony Financial highlights its consumer finance role as a key U.S. credit provider
articles.summary: Synchrony Financial plays a central role in U.S. consumer finance through private-label credit cards and installment loans, deeply tied to retail spending and digital payment trends. The company partners with various merchants to offer tailored financing, managing underwriting...
articles.content:
Synchrony Financial is a major U.S. consumer financial services company, best known for its private-label credit card and installment lending partnerships with large retailers and service providers. The company (ISIN US87165B1035) focuses on consumer credit solutions that support retail purchases, healthcare expenses, and everyday financing needs, making it an important player in the American credit ecosystem.

Focus on consumer credit partnerships

At the core of Synchrony Financial's business model is its role as a specialist in co-branded and private-label credit cards. It partners with a wide range of merchants across retail, home improvement, automotive services, and healthcare to offer tailored financing programs to their customers. These programs often provide promotional financing options, such as deferred interest or fixed-payment plans over a set period, which can help consumers manage larger purchases more flexibly.

The company typically assumes responsibility for underwriting, servicing, and managing these accounts, while retail partners focus on sales and customer relationships. This structure allows merchants to offer financing without building a full-scale banking operation, while Synchrony Financial earns interest income and fees from revolving balances and new loan originations. The model ties the company's performance closely to trends in consumer spending, credit quality, and retail sales cycles in the United States.

Digital platforms and data-driven risk management

Synchrony Financial has invested in digital platforms to support account opening, servicing, and payment management across web and mobile channels. Consumers can apply for credit, check balances, make payments, and manage rewards online, reflecting the broader shift in financial services toward self-service digital experiences. For retailers, these capabilities can integrate with existing point-of-sale systems and e-commerce platforms, enabling seamless checkout experiences where financing is offered as part of the purchase flow.

Risk management is a critical part of the company's operations. Synchrony Financial uses data-driven credit models to assess applicants, monitor portfolio performance, and adjust underwriting standards as economic conditions change. Factors such as employment trends, household debt levels, interest rate movements, and inflation all influence consumer repayment behavior, so the company continuously evaluates credit risk to maintain portfolio quality. This includes managing charge-offs, delinquency rates, and reserves for credit losses as part of its financial planning.

Representative consumer financing offering

A representative offering from Synchrony Financial is its private-label credit card programs with large retail chains. These cards are designed to encourage repeat purchases and build customer loyalty by providing rewards, promotional financing for big-ticket items, and account management tools. From a consumer perspective, such cards can make it easier to spread the cost of appliances, furniture, electronics, or home improvement projects over time, subject to approval and the card's specific terms.

On the merchant side, these programs can increase average transaction size and support marketing campaigns that highlight financing as part of the value proposition. Synchrony Financial's systems typically integrate with checkout processes in-store and online, allowing customers to apply for credit at the point of sale and receive decisions quickly. This combination of financing, technology integration, and customer analytics is central to the company's competitive positioning.

Stock trading and market context

Synchrony Financial stock trades in the United States and reflects investor expectations about consumer credit trends, retail spending, and the broader economic outlook. The shares are influenced by factors such as interest rate policy, household income growth, and competitive dynamics in the credit card and lending markets. When economic conditions support robust consumer activity and stable credit performance, investor sentiment toward consumer finance companies can be more favorable; periods of rising delinquencies or macro uncertainty typically lead to more cautious positioning.

For investors, key metrics often include loan growth, net interest margin, credit loss trends, and efficiency ratios, along with the diversification of merchant partnerships across sectors such as retail, healthcare, and services. Earnings reports and regulatory filings provide detailed insights into these areas, although specific current figures are not referenced here. The stock remains closely tied to U.S. consumer finance cycles and competitive developments in payment and lending technologies.

articles.title: Huntington Ingalls highlights its naval shipbuilding role as defense demand stays firm
articles.summary: Huntington Ingalls, a major U.S. defense contractor, continues to focus on large naval programs, benefiting from resilient military spending and long-term shipbuilding contracts. The company is the largest military shipbuilder in the U.S., providing advanced warships and lifec...
articles.content:
Huntington Ingalls (ISIN US4464131063) is the largest military shipbuilder in the United States and a key contractor for the U.S. Navy, delivering aircraft carriers, amphibious assault ships and advanced destroyers for long-term defense programs. The company is listed in the U.S. equity market and its business is closely tied to the outlook for defense spending and fleet modernization initiatives.

Shipbuilding backbone for the U.S. Navy

The core of Huntington Ingalls' business model is the design, construction and lifecycle support of complex warships for the U.S. Navy and other government customers. Its shipyards build and overhaul major surface vessels and amphibious platforms that form the backbone of U.S. naval power projection and sealift capability.

These programs are typically awarded under long-duration government contracts, which provide relatively stable revenue streams and help smooth out short-term fluctuations in defense procurement cycles. Multi-ship contracts and block buys can lock in work for years, giving the company visibility into future workload and supporting planning for labor, materials and capital investment.

Long-term contracts and margin focus

For investors, one key theme in Huntington Ingalls' story is the balance between long-term contract stability and the operational challenges of executing highly complex projects. Building advanced warships involves substantial engineering work, strict regulatory oversight and coordination across large supply chains, making cost control and schedule management central to profitability.

Analysts often focus on contract margins, performance incentives and potential cost overruns when assessing the company's earnings power. Efficient execution on major programs can support healthy operating margins, while delays or unexpected technical issues can weigh on profitability and cash flow. Over time, experience across similar ship classes can help refine processes and improve productivity.

Representative programs and capabilities

A representative part of Huntington Ingalls' portfolio is its work on large deck amphibious assault ships and other complex surface vessels. These ships are designed to support Marine Corps operations, humanitarian missions and joint-force deployments, combining aviation facilities, troop capacity and logistics support in a single platform.

The company also provides lifecycle services such as maintenance, modernization and technical support for ships already in service. This work extends the economic life of vessels, keeps them aligned with evolving mission requirements and can generate recurring revenue beyond the initial construction phase. Engineering, design and digital solutions offered alongside shipbuilding deepen customer relationships and may open additional service opportunities.

Huntington Ingalls stock and investor perspective

Huntington Ingalls stock trades on a major U.S. exchange, and investors often view it through the lens of defense spending trends, program execution and free cash flow generation. The share price reflects expectations for long-term demand for naval platforms, the timing of large contract awards and the company's ability to manage costs on complex projects.

Beyond individual quarters, the long lead times and multi-year nature of naval shipbuilding mean that Huntington Ingalls' equity story is closely linked to sustained government investment in fleet modernization and readiness rather than short-lived cycles. For investors, the interplay between contract backlog, margins and capital allocation remains central to assessing the stock's risk and reward profile.

articles.summary: Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE: GS) has significantly outperformed the market over the last five years, with an annualized return of 22.52%. An initial investment of $1,000 in GS stock five years ago would now be worth $2,760.50. This highlights the powerful effect of compounded re...
articles.content:
Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 11.06% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 22.52%. Currently, Goldman Sachs Group has a market capitalization of $301.19 billion.

Buying $1000 In GS: If an investor had bought $1000 of GS stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $2,760.50 today based on a price of $1021.00 for GS at the time of writing.

Goldman Sachs Group’s Performance Over Last 5 Years

Finally -- what’s the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

articles.title: EU crypto rulebook faces enforcement challenge as MiCA transition ends
articles.summary: Lawyers and industry executives expect EU regulators to enforce MiCA differently as unauthorized crypto companies are required to wind down operations.
articles.content:
EU crypto rulebook faces enforcement challenge as MiCA transition ends

Lawyers and industry executives expect EU regulators to enforce MiCA differently as unauthorized crypto companies are required to wind down operations.

The European Union’s cryptocurrency industry has entered a new enforcement phase as the transition period under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation came to an end.

The end of the transition means crypto companies without MiCA authorization can no longer legally serve EU clients and are expected to wind down operations or face multimillion-euro fines and other enforcement action.

Industry executives and lawyers told Cointelegraph the next challenge is ensuring national regulators apply the bloc’s single rulebook consistently, even as supervisory approaches are expected to vary across member states.

The transition marks MiCA’s first major enforcement test as regulators begin applying the EU's crypto rulebook.

MiCA compliance costs versus fines

Although complying with MiCA can cost hundreds of thousands or, in some cases, millions of euros, experts say operating without authorization carries far greater financial and regulatory risks.

Nicola Massella, partner at Storm Partners, estimated MiCA implementation costs for many cryptocurrency companies at 350,000 euros ($400,000) to 600,000 euros ($690,000), while Brickken CEO Edwin Mata said costs can reach 2 million euros ($2.3 million) depending on a company’s size, services and compliance readiness.

On penalties, Eckehard Stolz, managing director of Amina EU, said MiCA penalties start at 5 million euros or 5% of annual turnover for some violations.

Source: EBA

Massella added that the European Banking Authority (EBA) proposed on June 26 increasing penalties under certain regulatory regimes, including as much as 12.5% of annual turnover for some stablecoin-related breaches.

Who enforces MiCA?

While MiCA creates a single EU rulebook, day-to-day supervision is handled by national competent authorities (NCAs), which authorize, supervise and enforce the rules for crypto companies.

The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) coordinates supervision across member states and maintains the public register of authorized crypto-asset service providers, and the EBA directly oversees significant stablecoin issuers.

Source: ESMA

“At the EU level, ESMA plays an important coordination and supervisory-convergence role, especially to avoid regulatory arbitrage between member states,” Ivo Grlica, founder of GrlicaLaw and G Lab Advisors, told Cointelegraph.

“National regulators are only the first line of MiCA enforcement, but the legal consequences can spread into national courts and criminal-law systems if the underlying conduct causes harm,” he added.

Enforcement unlikely to be uniform at first

MiCA enforcement is unlikely to be uniform in its early stages because NCAs differ in resources, experience and supervisory priorities.

“ESMA made clear it expects NCAs to act against unauthorized providers from July 1,” Stolz said, adding that how aggressively each regulator moves “will depend on local resourcing and priorities.”

Peter Bidewell, vice president of institutional product adoption at Parfin, said differing supervisory approaches could create opportunities for regulatory arbitrage despite MiCA's goal of harmonizing crypto rules across the EU.

Related: StanChart joins ESMA's first MiCA register update since deadline

Grlica said he expects enforcement to become more systematic over time as regulators identify unauthorized providers and share information across member states, making it increasingly difficult for companies with a history of non-compliance to obtain MiCA authorization later.

Several EU regulators, including authorities in the Czech Republic, Bulgaria, Luxembourg and Italy, have issued notices reminding crypto companies that the MiCA transition period has ended and urging providers without authorization to wind down their operations.

The Czech National Bank told Cointelegraph that the country's Financial Market Digitization Act gives it the authority to impose sanctions for MiCA-related violations, including operating without authorization, unlawful token offerings and failing to cooperate with supervisors. The law allows the central bank to fine companies providing crypto services without authorization up to 118.5 million Czech koruna (about $5.6 million), 5% of annual turnover if higher, or twice the unlawful benefit obtained, whichever is greater.

Cointelegraph contacted France's Autorité des marchés financiers (AMF), the Netherlands’ Authority for the Financial Markets (AFM) and Germany's Federal Financial Supervisory Authority (BaFin) to ask how they plan to enforce MiCA following the transition deadline. None had responded by publication.

Magazine: How crypto laws changed in 2025 — and how they’ll change in 2026

More on the subject

articles.title: Halliburton Co outlook and operations context for US investors
articles.summary: This article provides an overview of Halliburton Co (HAL), a major oilfield services provider. It highlights how the company's stock is tied to trends in drilling activity, energy prices, and capital spending by exploration and production companies, offering US investors expos...
articles.content:
Halliburton Co is one of the largest global oilfield services companies, with a long history of supporting exploration and production activity for energy producers in North America and international markets. As a diversified service provider, Halliburton is closely tied to trends in drilling investment, well completions and reservoir optimization across conventional and unconventional resources.

For US investors, Halliburton is a key name in the upstream services space, providing exposure to activity levels in onshore shale basins and offshore developments. The company’s performance is influenced by energy prices, customer capital budgets and technology adoption in areas such as digital subsurface tools and advanced completion techniques. Halliburton’s stock often moves with expectations for future drilling intensity and service pricing in core regions.

Global oilfield services footprint

Halliburton operates a broad portfolio of oilfield services and products designed to support the full lifecycle of a well, from planning and drilling to completion and production optimization. This includes services for conventional reservoirs, deepwater projects and unconventional resource plays. Its footprint spans major producing regions, reflecting the global nature of upstream activity.

The company’s service mix ranges from drilling-related offerings, such as wellbore construction technologies and drilling fluids, to completion solutions like hydraulic fracturing, cementing and well stimulation. It also provides production enhancement services, reservoir testing and monitoring capabilities, and integrated project management for complex field developments. This breadth allows Halliburton to participate across multiple stages of customers’ field programs.

Industry and market context

Halliburton’s business model is closely linked to exploration and production spending cycles. When energy producers increase their budgets for drilling and completing wells, demand for oilfield services typically rises, supporting activity for companies like Halliburton. Conversely, periods of reduced capital spending can weigh on service volumes and pricing, making operating discipline and cost management important.

In recent years, industry participants have paid close attention to efficiency, returns on capital and the balance between growth and shareholder returns. This has influenced how customers plan their drilling programs and the kind of services they seek, including technologies that help lower costs per barrel and improve well performance. Halliburton’s positioning in completion technologies, subsurface solutions and digital tools is relevant to these evolving priorities.

Representative services and technology

Halliburton offers a wide range of services and technology solutions that are representative of its role in the oilfield services industry. Examples include directional drilling tools that help operators reach complex reservoir targets, cementing services that ensure well integrity, and hydraulic fracturing solutions used to stimulate production in tight oil and gas formations.

Beyond the physical services delivered on the wellsite, Halliburton has invested in software and digital platforms that assist customers in reservoir modeling, data interpretation and operational planning. These capabilities are designed to make drilling and completion programs more efficient and reduce nonproductive time. As energy producers look to leverage data and analytics, such tools become part of the broader value proposition of modern oilfield service companies.

Halliburton Co stock context

Halliburton Co stock provides investors with exposure to oilfield services activity and to trends in drilling and completion spending by exploration and production companies. The share price reflects expectations for future demand, service pricing, margin performance and the company’s ability to manage costs across cycles.

Because oilfield services are cyclical, investors often consider Halliburton’s stock in the context of broader energy-market expectations and capital-allocation decisions by its customers. Over time, the company’s scale, diversified service offering and global presence can play a role in how market participants view its resilience across industry cycles.
